subject: Move Cloudify's password update and plugins upload to start-persistent.sh script
commit_message:
Move Cloudify's password update and plugins upload to
start-persistent.sh script

Move logic for changing cloudify's admin password and plugin upload
to main script for debug purposes, so that output of those scripts
is printed to stdout (can be accessed by 'docker/kubernetes logs')

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2457
Signed-off-by: Remigiusz Janeczek <remigiusz.janeczek@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I69fb0b428efbfb3d85eba286ed6816151e03a0f1

entries.message:

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-deployments-master-verify-java/367/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

rc.local is hard to debug (would require creating another log file), my proposition is to move the scripts to docker entrypoint and add some logs.
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-deployments-master-verify-java/368/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-deployments-master-verify-java/368

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Like the change to move custom script execution to init; will wait for others to have chance to review. This could possibly gives some info w.r.t to integration issue DCAEGEN2-2457

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Thanks, Remigiusz. This is a big improvement.  Not only do we see the logs easily, but we get rid of the dependency on the rc.local compatibility mode.
Change has been successfully merged by Jack Lucas

Sadly there are some issues with multisite-init-container build, don't see any made changes there recently though. Same thing happens in: https://jenkins.onap.org/view/dcaegen2/job/dcaegen2-deployments-master-release-version-java-daily/ . Not sure how to proceed with the cm-container from here.
The job produced image anyway: onap/org.onap.dcaegen2.deployments.cm-container:3.3.3-SNAPSHOT-20200929T170041Z , which works fine in my environment.

Patch Set 2:

There is a problem with the multisite-init-container build.  It's reproducible outside of the Jenkins build environment.  I don't understand it yet, but will investigate further.  I've opened DCAEGEN2-2463 to track this.

As there is no change for multisite container for Guilin, this module could temporarily disabled  to allow CM container build/release.
